# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Deploy Node.js to AWS ECS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: demo # Optional: specify an environment, good practice for secrets later

    env:
      AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
      AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY
      AWS_REGION: us-east-1 # Or your desired AWS region
      ECR_REPOSITORY: my-nodejs-app # Name of your ECR repository
      ECS_CLUSTER: my-nodejs-cluster # Name of your ECS cluster
      ECS_SERVICE: my-nodejs-service # Name of your ECS service
      # For demonstration, a placeholder AWS account ID. Replace with your actual ID.
      AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: 123456789012 

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18' # Or your desired Node.js version

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to Amazon ECR
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Build and tag Docker image
        run: |
          docker build -t ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:latest .
          docker tag ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:latest ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:latest

      - name: Push Docker image to Amazon ECR
        run: docker push ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:latest

      - name: Deploy to Amazon ECS service
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecs-deploy@v1
        with:
          service: ${{ env.ECS_SERVICE }}
          cluster: ${{ env.ECS_CLUSTER }}
          image: ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:latest
          # For task definitions, if you're not using the `image` parameter for direct image update,
          # you would specify `task-definition` and `ecr-repository` or use the `aws ecs register-task-definition` CLI.
          # Example: task-definition: task-definition.json (if you have one in your repo)
